questions.q: In which field of Indian cinema is Ilaiyaraaja primarily renowned?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja is celebrated as one of India's most prolific film music composers, scoring more than a thousand films.

questions.q: By what honorific title is the composer Ilaiyaraaja widely known?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja is popularly called 'Isaignani', a Tamil honorific meaning 'musical genius' or 'sage of music'.

questions.q: Which 1976 Tamil film marked Ilaiyaraaja's debut as a film music composer?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja made his debut as a composer with the 1976 Tamil film Annakili, whose folk-rooted songs became hugely popular.

questions.q: In which Indian language did Ilaiyaraaja predominantly compose film music?
questions.explanation: Though he worked across several languages, Ilaiyaraaja composed predominantly for Tamil cinema.

questions.q: Which wind instrument serves as the emblem associated with Ilaiyaraaja in this deck and reflects his lyrical melodic style?
questions.explanation: The flute is the emblem associated with Ilaiyaraaja, reflecting his lyrical, melody-driven sensibility.

questions.q: Ilaiyaraaja is noted as among the first Indian film composers to employ what kind of large-scale orchestration?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja was among the first Indian composers to use full Western symphonic orchestration in film scores.

questions.q: From which decade did Ilaiyaraaja begin his dominant run as a film composer?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja's prolific career as a film composer began in the late 1970s following his 1976 debut.

questions.q: Roughly how many films has Ilaiyaraaja composed music for over his career?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja has scored music for more than a thousand films, making him one of the most prolific composers in world cinema.

questions.q: Two distinct Indian classical and folk traditions strongly shaped Ilaiyaraaja's style. Which pair best describes them?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja fused Tamil folk idioms and Carnatic music with Western classical orchestration.

questions.q: Which prestigious Indian civilian honour was conferred on the composer Ilaiyaraaja?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja was conferred the Padma Vibhushan, one of India's highest civilian honours.

questions.q: In which Indian state was the composer Ilaiyaraaja born?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja was born in 1943 in Pannaipuram in the Theni district of Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: In which year was the composer Ilaiyaraaja born?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja was born on 2 June 1943.

questions.q: Ilaiyaraaja's son, who also became a noted Tamil film composer and singer, is known by which name?
questions.explanation: Yuvan Shankar Raja, Ilaiyaraaja's son, is an acclaimed Tamil film composer in his own right.

questions.q: Which 1985 Tamil film about a Carnatic musician, directed by K. Balachander, earned Ilaiyaraaja a National Film Award for Best Music Direction?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja won the National Film Award for Best Music Direction for Sindhu Bhairavi (1985), K. Balachander's film steeped in Carnatic music.

questions.q: Ilaiyaraaja is often cited as the first Asian composer to record a full symphony with which renowned London orchestra?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja recorded a symphony with London's Royal Philharmonic Orchestra, widely noted as a first for an Asian composer.

questions.q: Which director did Ilaiyaraaja collaborate with on landmark 1980s Tamil films such as Mouna Ragam and Nayakan?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja scored several early Mani Ratnam films including Mouna Ragam (1986) and Nayakan (1987).

questions.q: Ilaiyaraaja's 1986 instrumental album exploring a fusion of Indian and Western classical music was titled what?
questions.explanation: His 1986 fusion album 'How To Name It?' blended Bach-influenced Western classical writing with Carnatic music dedicated to Tyagaraja.

questions.q: Which celebrated flautist featured prominently on Ilaiyaraaja's 1988 instrumental album 'Nothing But Wind'?
questions.explanation: The album 'Nothing But Wind' showcased flute maestro Hariprasad Chaurasia performing Ilaiyaraaja's compositions.

questions.q: In 2005, Ilaiyaraaja released an oratorio setting the Tamil devotional verses of Manikkavachakar to symphonic music. What was it titled?
questions.explanation: 'Thiruvasagam in Symphony' (2005) set the ancient Tamil Saiva hymns of Manikkavachakar to a full symphonic score.

questions.q: Which playback singer formed a famous partnership with Ilaiyaraaja, singing many of his hit Tamil songs from the late 1970s onward?
questions.explanation: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam was Ilaiyaraaja's frequent male voice, delivering countless hits across their long collaboration.

questions.q: Before the Padma Vibhushan, which other Padma award did Ilaiyaraaja receive in 2010?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja was awarded the Padma Bhushan in 2010 and later the Padma Vibhushan in 2018.

questions.q: In 2022, Ilaiyaraaja was nominated to which body of the Indian Parliament?
questions.explanation: In 2022 Ilaiyaraaja was nominated to the Rajya Sabha, the upper house of India's Parliament.

questions.q: Which 1978 Tamil film directed by Mahendran, starring Rajinikanth with a celebrated Ilaiyaraaja score, is regarded as an early classic of his music?
questions.explanation: Mullum Malarum (1978), directed by Mahendran with an acclaimed Ilaiyaraaja score, starred Rajinikanth.

questions.q: Ilaiyaraaja studied Western classical music through a correspondence course associated with which London institution?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja earned a diploma in classical guitar through a correspondence course from Trinity College of Music, London.

questions.q: Which of these is the birth name of the composer later known as Ilaiyaraaja?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja was born Gnanathesigan (also spelled Gnanadesikan); the name Ilaiyaraaja was given to him at the start of his film career in 1976.

questions.q: For which 1983 Telugu film directed by K. Viswanath did Ilaiyaraaja win a National Film Award for Best Music Direction?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja won the National Film Award for Best Music Direction for Sagara Sangamam (1983), directed by K. Viswanath and starring Kamal Haasan.

questions.q: Ilaiyaraaja's brothers were also musicians; which brother was a lyricist and music director who worked alongside him early on?
questions.explanation: Gangai Amaran, Ilaiyaraaja's brother, was himself a music director and lyricist.

questions.q: In which of these film industries did Ilaiyaraaja NOT work as a regular film composer during his peak?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja composed extensively for Tamil, Telugu, Kannada and Malayalam films, but not for Hollywood studio productions.

questions.q: Which 1982 Tamil film starring Kamal Haasan and Sridevi, directed by Balu Mahendra, had a beloved Ilaiyaraaja score?
questions.explanation: Moondram Pirai (1982), directed by Balu Mahendra and starring Kamal Haasan and Sridevi, featured one of Ilaiyaraaja's most cherished scores.

questions.q: In which National Film Award category has Ilaiyaraaja won multiple times for his film work?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja won the National Film Award for Best Music Direction multiple times, including for Sagara Sangamam, Sindhu Bhairavi and Rudraveena.
